criterion: Reverts speed, jump, color, and enemy collision behavior by the next physics frame after the timer ends.

forms.assessment_process: Trigger the power up, wait for expiry, then verify all four behaviors match baseline on the next physics tick. Confirm no errors occur when the timer ends.
forms.explanation: Clean state transitions prevent hard to debug edge cases and ensure the player experience matches expectations when the effect ends.

criterion: Includes at least two collectible_powerup instances in platformer.tscn, placed near enemies or on elevated routes.

forms.assessment_process: Open platformer.tscn and count collectible_powerup nodes, then confirm their positions are adjacent to enemy areas or higher platforms.
forms.explanation: Intentional placement ensures the mechanic is discoverable and provides meaningful gameplay value rather than being purely cosmetic.

criterion: Does not change the score value or score label when collectible_powerup is collected.

forms.assessment_process: Collect a power up and compare the score value and label text before and after pickup.
forms.explanation: Separating mechanics avoids unintended progression changes and prevents regressions in the existing scoring system.

criterion: Uses a single boolean in player.gd to gate speed, jump, color, and enemy contact outcomes.

forms.assessment_process: Inspect player.gd for one flag that controls all four behaviors via conditional checks or a centralized state function.
forms.explanation: Centralized state reduces bugs, makes timer resets reliable, and keeps the feature extensible for future power ups.

criterion: Exposes an exported duration on collectible_powerup that is editable per instance in the Inspector.

forms.assessment_process: Select different collectible_powerup instances in platformer.tscn and confirm each can set a different duration value.
forms.explanation: Per instance tuning supports level design iteration without code changes and keeps balancing fast and safe.

criterion: Triggers the original player death and respawn behavior on enemy contact when power up is inactive.

forms.assessment_process: Run the game, avoid collecting a power up, touch chaser and shooter enemies, and confirm baseline death and respawn occurs.
forms.explanation: Regression protection ensures the new mechanic does not break core fail states and preserves expected difficulty.

criterion: Removes chaser and shooter enemies on player contact while power up is active, without killing the player.

forms.assessment_process: Collect a power up, collide with each enemy type, and confirm the enemy disappears and the player remains alive.
forms.explanation: This is the core gameplay reversal of the power up and must be reliable for player trust and level balance.

criterion: Increases jump height during power up and restores the exact baseline jump value after expiry.

forms.assessment_process: Compare max jump height with and without power up, then inspect the jump variable value before pickup and after expiry.
forms.explanation: Jump boost is a primary reward signal and must revert cleanly to avoid breaking traversal and level gating.

criterion: Increases horizontal speed during power up and restores the exact baseline speed value after expiry.

forms.assessment_process: Measure movement speed or inspect the speed variable while active, then confirm it matches baseline after the timer ends.
forms.explanation: Speed boosts affect combat and platforming timing, so exact restoration prevents unintended difficulty shifts.

criterion: Resets the remaining power up time to full duration on re pickup, without reverting player color in between.

forms.assessment_process: Collect a power up, collect another before expiry, and confirm the timer extends and the player color stays in the powered state.
forms.explanation: Timer reset behavior prevents confusing feedback and ensures consistent rules when chaining pickups.

criterion: Restores the player visual color to its exact pre power up value when the effect expires.

forms.assessment_process: Record the player color before pickup, then confirm the same value is restored after expiry and after a re pickup chain.
forms.explanation: Clear visual state prevents player confusion and avoids persistent UI bugs that imply the effect is still active.

criterion: Changes the player visual color immediately on power up pickup, and the new color is clearly distinct.

forms.assessment_process: Collect a power up and confirm the player color changes on the same moment as pickup and is visually different from baseline.
forms.explanation: Immediate feedback communicates the temporary state and helps players understand why collisions and movement changed.

criterion: Displays collectible_powerup with a different color and larger size than the regular collectible.

forms.assessment_process: Run the scene and visually compare the two collectible types for color difference and size difference.
forms.explanation: Distinct visuals reduce misreads during fast play and prevent players from confusing power ups with score pickups.

criterion: Includes collectible_powerup.tscn and collectible_powerup.gd, and matches the regular collectible Area2D structure.

forms.assessment_process: Verify the files exist in the project and open the scene to confirm it is an Area2D based collectible similar to the regular one.
forms.explanation: Consistent scene structure improves maintainability and ensures the new collectible integrates with existing pickup logic.

criterion: Runs in Godot 4.x with no new errors, and no new warnings introduced by the power up changes.

forms.assessment_process: Run the project and compare the debugger output to the baseline branch or prior commit, focusing on new messages from modified files.
forms.explanation: Keeping the project clean prevents hidden runtime failures and ensures the feature is shippable and easy to debug.
